title: Reading the Question Too Fast
problem: Students often answer what they expected the question to ask, not what it actually asks.
fix: Circle or mentally mark command words such as nearest, opposite, except, best, main and inference.

title: Ignoring Time Until the Final Week
problem: Accuracy may look good in untimed practice but collapse during timed exams.
fix: Add short timed sets from the beginning, then move to section mocks and full mocks.

title: Using Formulas Without Concept
problem: Students memorize formulas but fail when the wording changes.
fix: After each formula, solve one direct question and one word problem using the same idea.

title: Practising Without Reviewing Mistakes
problem: The same wrong pattern repeats because the student moves to new questions too quickly.
fix: Write every repeated mistake under topic, reason and correct method.

title: Random Vocabulary Memorization
problem: Students memorize lists but cannot use words in context.
fix: Learn each word with one sentence, one synonym, one antonym and one related word.

title: Starting Mocks Too Late
problem: Students wait until the syllabus is perfect, then discover timing and stamina problems too late.
fix: Start mini mocks early. Full mocks can come later, but timed exposure should begin now.
